title: networks/list.go updates for --format
body:
Podman Network ls --format changes

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YwNkk7t9o-MUbIDcvSMFXkWlnKR-OlGjwD_L5axKx50

Has an issue with 'make validate':

cmd/podman/networks/list.go:97:3: ineffectual assignment to `format` (ineffassign)
		format = report.NormalizeFormat(networkListOptions.Format)
		^
cmd/podman/networks/list.go:99:12: ineffectual assignment to `format` (ineffassign)
		headers, format = createListOut()

However I was following another file (https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/7973/commits/e9b667bb5f9c72fd9903bfa5efd081ad24a2482b podman containers ps) which does not receive this problem
Also - this does not use networkListOptions in createListOut, does it need to?

Signed-off-by: Parker Van Roy <pvanroy@redhat.com>

comments.body:
wip, current code changes break :
➜  podman git:(format-networks/list) podman network ls
NAME    VERSION  PLUGINS
Error: template: listNetworks:2:2: executing "listNetworks" at <.Name>: can't evaluate field Name in type []network.ListPrintReports
@ParkerVR Check that  you have `{{range .}}...{end}} ` around the template where .Name is not found

> @ParkerVR Check that you have `{{range .}}...{end}} ` around the template where .Name is not found

thanks for the advice, I think this should be properly fixed. I don't have any networks to test with, can you give instructions on how to generate some examples?
Does the new containers/common with format fixes fix this PR?
@rhatdan  they should work together, ie this pr should use the containers/common format fixes
and please remove the `[WIP]` in the title before merging.
Also if you are rebasing could you squash commits and change dwalsh->rhatdan or don't mention me at all.
@ParkerVR What is the scoop on this one?
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
made a mistake rebasing friday that added some extraneous files, will get it rebased after I get out of class today

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Against master, to an already-tagged version? I see no problem.

/lgtm
